steps.title: Set the equations equal
steps.content: Since both expressions equal y, we set them equal: $$x^2 - 4x + 4 = 4 - x$$

steps.title: Simplify to standard form
steps.content: Move all terms to the left side. Add x to both sides and subtract 4 from both sides: $$x^2 - 4x + x + 4 - 4 = 0$$ $$x^2 - 3x = 0$$

steps.title: Factor and find the solutions
steps.content: Factor out the common factor x: $$x(x - 3) = 0$$ This gives us x = 0 or x = 3. Either value satisfies both equations.
